source_note: Calendar-defined 2025-01-01 to 2050-12-31 horizon (26 years); this is an analysis-design choice, not a literature-estimated parameter.
Initialization choice used to reduce dependence on arbitrary starting infections and immunity; not a directly cited biological parameter.
Baseline placeholder in the shared config; country-specific beta_S is calibrated to reported surveillance and reported separately in eTable 8.
WHO/aP evidence supports residual infection and transmission under aP-like protection, but not this exact ratio; retained as a sensitivity parameter.
Country profiles infer 3-5-year periodicity from annual surveillance peaks when visible; otherwise use 4 years.
Weak phase-locking term disabled by default; main interpretation remains susceptible depletion/replenishment plus seasonal forcing.
Uses 8 days within CDC's common 7-10 day incubation range.
Uses 21 days, aligned with infectiousness through roughly the third week after paroxysm onset.
Shorter duration for mild/asymptomatic infections is a modeling assumption anchored to the symptomatic infectious window; sensitivity range retained.

source_or_assumption: CDC clinical guidance and cycle-model evidence
source_note: Uses 9 years within CDC's 4-20 year observational range; longer natural-immunity values remain sensitivity cases within the current SIRWS waning-and-boosting structure.
Uses 5 years to approximate rapid aP waning; meta-analysis shows marked decline by 3-7 years.

description: Daily transition from symptomatic infection to treatment
source_note: CDC supports early macrolide treatment but does not define the daily treatment transition rate; numeric treatment effects are implementation assumptions evaluated in sensitivity/scenario analyses.

description: Dynamic PEP coverage ceiling among close contacts
source_note: CDC supports PEP for household/high-risk contacts but does not define this coverage ceiling; the numeric value approximates reach in a model without explicit households.
